title: SierraSlides: AI Feedback for Professional Slide Revision
conference: Designed and implemented a Google Slides add-on that generates context-specific AI feedback varying in tone (critical to positive) and assistance level (direct suggestions vs. reflective questions). Conducted a formative study (N=11) and a technology probe study (N=13) with product managers using their real-world slide decks to examine how AI feedback shapes reflection and preserves agency in professional workflows.

title: Metacognitive Self-Revision for AI Agents
conference: Implemented a two-level metacognitive self-revision architecture integrating symbolic knowledge and LLM reasoning to localize, revise, and explain errors in LLM-based AI agents. Deployed the architecture in a live course with 500+ users and evaluated whether transparent self-revision maintains user trust.

title: Stupid Tutor: Trust Calibration Under LLM Inconsistency
conference:
Designed and conducted a controlled online experiment (N=252) to examine how LLM inconsistency affects perceived AI capacity and user comprehension. Led statistical analysis in Python and R (regression modeling, hypothesis testing), finding that inconsistency can serve as a mechanism for calibrating trust and reducing overreliance.
• Built full-stack experimental infrastructure (Flask, SQLite, JavaScript, HTML) supporting random assignment, response logging, and data collection.

title: VAL: Interactive Task Planning Interface
conference: Developed a React-based interactive GUI for VAL, an HTN (Hierarchical Task Network) planning agent, enabling users to review, edit, and approve AI-generated task decompositions in real time via WebSocket communication.

title: AI-Augmented Financial Auditing
conference: Developed and deployed AI systems to augment financial auditors' workflows, enabling them to surface more claims and prioritize high-risk cases without replacing auditor judgment. Integrated domain expert feedback through weekly sessions to translate domain expertise into model features and decision criteria.

title: Explainable Churn Prediction for Service Teams
conference: Built a customer churn prediction model (XGBoost) on multi-terabyte datasets with SHAP-based explanations, enabling service team members to interpret model outputs and take targeted retention actions. Developed a SQL-based customer lifecycle tracking pipeline with dashboards, reducing upstream data inconsistencies by 93.9%.

title: Real-Time Background Segmentation
conference: Developed and deployed a real-time background segmentation model (PyTorch) for video conferencing, achieving 1.5× speed, 34% higher accuracy, and 14% fewer parameters than the baseline.

title: LTE Network KPI Analysis
conference: Analyzed KPI correlations on LTE eNB datasets to assess predictability between input and output performance indicators.

title: Sound Clouds: Participatory Music Generation
conference: Developed an object detection model by fine-tuning YOLO12n, achieving 98.3% accuracy in tracking inflatable movements. Conducted data analysis and visualization (N=35) using Python and Pandas.

title: FORECST: Online Hackathon Platform
conference: Built a full-stack hackathon website with React.js and Firebase in a team of 4, enabling virtual hackathons for novice programmers during COVID-19.

title: PINTOS: Operating System Implementation
conference: Implemented kernel threads, user program execution, file system, and virtual memory for the 80x86 architecture in C, tested on the QEMU simulator.

title: MYFIT: Clothing Size Recommendation App
conference: Built an Android application in Java in a team of 3 to enhance online clothing shopping by visualizing clothing sizes on user body measurements.
